mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% signals severe labor market distress and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC greater than 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows, or housing and consumption data diverge without affecting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in over 60% of months where INDPRO rises, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside the post-2009 recovery phase.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the consumer price index above 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 50% of instances following CPI spikes above the threshold, or the negative correlation vanishes when controlling for Fed funds rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a synchronized labor market recovery, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or payrolls rise while unemployment falls without generating positive forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained economic expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in industrial production indicates expanding factory output and supply chain efficiency, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: The trend signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.
